summary: time-format-kit postinstall exfiltrates SSH/AWS credentials and shell history to attacker OOB endpoint
details: Package presents itself as a dayjs helper but the postinstall lifecycle script runs an obfuscated payload that decodes base64 shell blobs and executes them via child_process.exec. The require target and method are constructed with string concatenation ('ch'+'il'+'d_p'+'rocess' and 'e'+'xec') to evade static scanners. The decoded shell reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, authorized_keys, known_hosts, ~/.bash_history, the full process environment, and host reconnaissance output (hostname, whoami, id, sudo -l), then base64-encodes the collected data and curls it over plain HTTP to a Burp Collaborator subdomain at pwpzhsrbtvmfqrqr7onqcwnrcii960up.oastify.com/ing. A second decoded payload issues SSRF probes to the internal host tst.woa.com and posts the response to the same collaborator infrastructure. The behavior fires automatically on npm install; the dayjs-helper description is a cover story.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
